title: CONTRASTIVE SELF-SUPERVISED DATA FUSION FOR SATELLITE IMAGERY
venue: ISPRS Annals of the Photogrammetry, Remote Sensing and Spatial Information Sciences
tldr: The paper proposes augmentation-free contrastive self-supervised data fusion for remote sensing by aligning Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 representations from the same location. Its main method, D-SimCLR, learns strong multimodal encoders from unlabeled data and outperforms supervised fusion baselines, especially when labeled data is scarce.
abstract: Abstract. Self-supervised learning has great potential for the remote sensing domain, where unlabelled observations are abundant, but labels are hard to obtain. This work leverages unlabelled multi-modal remote sensing data for augmentation-free contrastive self-supervised learning. Deep neural network models are trained to maximize the similarity of latent representations obtained with different sensing techniques from the same location, while distinguishing them from other locations. We showcase this idea with two self-supervised data fusion methods and compare against standard supervised and self-supervised learning approaches on a land-cover classification task. Our results show that contrastive data fusion is a powerful self-supervised technique to train image encoders that are capable of producing meaningful representations: Simple linear probing performs on par with fully supervised approaches and fine-tuning with as little as 10% of the labelled data results in higher accuracy than supervised training on the entire dataset.
key_contribution: The paper proposes augmentation-free contrastive self-supervised data fusion for remote sensing by aligning Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 representations from the same location. Its main method, D-SimCLR, learns strong multimodal encoders from unlabeled data and outperforms supervised fusion baselines, especially when labeled data is scarce.

pretraining.objective: contrastive alignment of cross-modal representations for the same geo-location

title: Geographical Supervision Correction for Remote Sensing Representation Learning
venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
tldr: The paper proposes GeCo, a remote sensing representation learning method that corrects noisy geographical supervision derived from global land cover products during pretraining. Its main novelty is a jointly optimized correction matrix constrained by land-cover change and spatial aggregation priors, which improves transfer performance across classification, segmentation, cloud/snow detection, and object detection.
key_contribution: The paper proposes GeCo, a remote sensing representation learning method that corrects noisy geographical supervision derived from global land cover products during pretraining. Its main novelty is a jointly optimized correction matrix constrained by land-cover change and spatial aggregation priors, which improves transfer performance across classification, segmentation, cloud/snow detection, and object detection.

architecture.type: encoder-only mean-teacher framework with projection head

pretraining.method: geographical supervision-based pretraining with adaptive correction
pretraining.objective: predict corrected geographical supervision (land-cover proportion vector) with MSE, plus student-teacher consistency and regularization losses

pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: mixed (remote sensing images at 0.8m, 2m, 16m; GLC products at 30m)
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: global for GLC products; Levir-KR coverage unclear

title: Self-Supervised Learning for Invariant Representations from Multi-Spectral and SAR Images
venue: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
tldr: The paper proposes RS-BYOL, a BYOL-based self-supervised remote sensing model that learns invariant representations by aligning multi-spectral and SAR views of the same location. It shows that remote-sensing-specific SSL pretraining, especially with joint MS-SAR data, outperforms ImageNet-based transfer and that even single-band pretraining can yield strong downstream performance.
abstract: Self-Supervised learning (SSL) has become the new state-of-art in several domain classification and segmentation tasks. Of these, one popular category in SSL is distillation networks such as BYOL. This work proposes RSDnet, which applies the distillation network (BYOL) in the remote sensing (RS) domain where data is non-trivially different from natural RGB images. Since Multi-spectral (MS) and synthetic aperture radar (SAR) sensors provide varied spectral and spatial resolution information, we utilised them as an implicit augmentation to learn invariant feature embeddings. In order to learn RS based invariant features with SSL, we trained RSDnet in two ways, i.e., single channel feature learning and three channel feature learning. This work explores the usefulness of single channel feature learning from random MS and SAR bands compared to the common notion of using three or more bands. In our linear evaluation, these single channel features reached a 0.92 F1 score on the EuroSAT classification task and 59.6 mIoU on the DFC segmentation task for certain single bands. We also compared our results with ImageNet weights and showed that the RS based SSL model outperforms the supervised ImageNet based model. We further explored the usefulness of multi-modal data compared to single modality data, and it is shown that utilising MS and SAR data learn better invariant representations than utilising only MS data.
key_contribution: The paper proposes RS-BYOL, a BYOL-based self-supervised remote sensing model that learns invariant representations by aligning multi-spectral and SAR views of the same location. It shows that remote-sensing-specific SSL pretraining, especially with joint MS-SAR data, outperforms ImageNet-based transfer and that even single-band pretraining can yield strong downstream performance.

architecture.type: student-teacher distillation / Siamese encoder with BYOL-style predictor-projector

pretraining.objective: Alignment of positive pairs via BYOL loss between student prediction and teacher projection across MS and SAR views; effectively invariant representation learning across modalities

pretraining_data.num_images: 90K pairs (50% of 180,662 triplets); also a 150K-pair variant

title: RingMo-Aerial: An Aerial Remote Sensing Foundation Model With Affine Transformation Contrastive Learning
venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
tldr: The paper proposes RingMo-Aerial, presented as the first aerial remote sensing foundation model, tailored to aerial imagery with oblique views, scale variation, and dense small objects. Its main innovations are FE-MSA for frequency-enhanced small-object representation, affine transformation contrastive pretraining (CLAF) for aerial viewpoint adaptation, and ARS-Adapter for efficient fine-tuning across diverse downstream ARS tasks.
abstract: Aerial Remote Sensing (ARS) vision tasks present significant challenges due to the unique viewing angle characteristics. Existing research has primarily focused on algorithms for specific tasks, which have limited applicability in a broad range of ARS vision applications. This paper proposes RingMo-Aerial, aiming to fill the gap in foundation model research in the field of ARS vision. A Frequency-Enhanced Multi-Head Self-Attention (FE-MSA) mechanism is introduced to strengthen the model's capacity for small-object representation. Complementarily, an affine transformation-based contrastive learning method improves its adaptability to the tilted viewing angles inherent in ARS tasks. Furthermore, the ARS-Adapter, an efficient parameter fine-tuning method, is proposed to improve the model's adaptability and performance in various ARS vision tasks. Experimental results demonstrate that RingMo-Aerial achieves SOTA performance on multiple downstream tasks. This indicates the practicality and efficacy of RingMo-Aerial in enhancing the performance of ARS vision tasks.
key_contribution: The paper proposes RingMo-Aerial, presented as the first aerial remote sensing foundation model, tailored to aerial imagery with oblique views, scale variation, and dense small objects. Its main innovations are FE-MSA for frequency-enhanced small-object representation, affine transformation contrastive pretraining (CLAF) for aerial viewpoint adaptation, and ARS-Adapter for efficient fine-tuning across diverse downstream ARS tasks.

pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction and contrastive alignment of original vs affine-transformed aerial images

datasets: custom aerial remote sensing pretraining dataset compiled from multiple collected open-source airborne remote sensing datasets

title: RingMo-lite: A Remote Sensing Multi-task Lightweight Network with CNN-Transformer Hybrid Framework
venue: arXiv.org
tldr: The paper introduces RingMo-lite, a lightweight remote sensing foundation model that combines CNN and Swin Transformer branches to explicitly model high-frequency local details and low-frequency global context. It also proposes FD-MIM, a frequency-domain masked image modeling pretraining strategy, enabling strong multi-task performance across classification, detection, segmentation, and change detection while reducing parameters by over 60% relative to RingMo.
abstract: In recent years, remote sensing (RS) vision foundation models such as RingMo have emerged and achieved excellent performance in various downstream tasks. However, the high demand for computing resources limits the application of these models on edge devices. It is necessary to design a more lightweight foundation model to support on-orbit RS image interpretation. Existing methods face challenges in achieving lightweight solutions while retaining generalization in RS image interpretation. This is due to the complex high and low-frequency spectral components in RS images, which make traditional single CNN or Vision Transformer methods unsuitable for the task. Therefore, this paper proposes RingMo-lite, an RS multi-task lightweight network with a CNN-Transformer hybrid framework, which effectively exploits the frequency-domain properties of RS to optimize the interpretation process. It is combined by the Transformer module as a low-pass filter to extract global features of RS images through a dual-branch structure, and the CNN module as a stacked high-pass filter to extract fine-grained details effectively. Furthermore, in the pretraining stage, the designed frequency-domain masked image modeling (FD-MIM) combines each image patch's high-frequency and low-frequency characteristics, effectively capturing the latent feature representation in RS data. As shown in Fig. 1, compared with RingMo, the proposed RingMo-lite reduces the parameters over 60% in various RS image interpretation tasks, the average accuracy drops by less than 2% in most of the scenes and achieves SOTA performance compared to models of the similar size. In addition, our work will be integrated into the MindSpore computing platform in the near future.
key_contribution: The paper introduces RingMo-lite, a lightweight remote sensing foundation model that combines CNN and Swin Transformer branches to explicitly model high-frequency local details and low-frequency global context. It also proposes FD-MIM, a frequency-domain masked image modeling pretraining strategy, enabling strong multi-task performance across classification, detection, segmentation, and change detection while reducing parameters by over 60% relative to RingMo.

pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction with L1 regression loss

datasets: custom visible remote sensing image dataset

title: SELF-SUPERVISED VISION TRANSFORMERS FOR JOINT SAR-OPTICAL REPRESENTATION LEARNING
venue: IEEE International Geoscience and Remote Sensing Symposium
tldr: The paper proposes DINO-MM, a multimodal self-supervised learning method for joint SAR-optical representation learning using vision transformers. Its key innovation is RandomSensorDrop, which enables a single pretrained model to learn robust representations from SAR-only, optical-only, or fused inputs and improves transfer even to single-modality downstream tasks.
abstract: Self-supervised learning (SSL) has attracted much interest in remote sensing and Earth observation due to its ability to learn task-agnostic representations without human annotation. While most of the existing SSL works in remote sensing utilize ConvNet backbones and focus on a single modality, we explore the potential of vision transformers (ViTs) for joint SAR-optical representation learning. Based on DINO, a state-of-the-art SSL algorithm that distills knowledge from two augmented views of an input image, we combine SAR and optical imagery by concatenating all channels to a unified input. Subsequently, we randomly mask out channels of one modality as a data augmentation strategy. While training, the model gets fed optical-only, SAR-only, and SAR-optical image pairs learning both inner- and intra-modality representations. Experimental results employing the BigEarthNet-MM dataset demonstrate the benefits of both, the ViT backbones and the proposed multimodal SSL algorithm DINO-MM.
key_contribution: The paper proposes DINO-MM, a multimodal self-supervised learning method for joint SAR-optical representation learning using vision transformers. Its key innovation is RandomSensorDrop, which enables a single pretrained model to learn robust representations from SAR-only, optical-only, or fused inputs and improves transfer even to single-modality downstream tasks.

pretraining.objective: Contrastive/self-distillation alignment of augmented views using cross-entropy between teacher and student outputs

affiliations: Deutsches Zentrum für Luft- und Raumfahrt e. V. (DLR)

title: Self-supervised Audiovisual Representation Learning for Remote Sensing Data
model_name: Self-supervised audiovisual representation learning with Batch Triplet Loss
venue: International Journal of Applied Earth Observation and Geoinformation
tldr: The paper introduces SoundingEarth, a large-scale dataset of 50K+ co-located aerial image and audio pairs, and proposes a self-supervised audiovisual pretraining framework for remote sensing. By aligning image and audio embeddings with a batch triplet loss, the learned visual backbones outperform common initialization and prior self-supervised baselines on multiple remote sensing downstream tasks.
abstract: Many current deep learning approaches make extensive use of backbone networks pre-trained on large datasets like ImageNet, which are then fine-tuned to perform a certain task. In remote sensing, the lack of comparable large annotated datasets and the wide diversity of sensing platforms impedes similar developments. In order to contribute towards the availability of pre-trained backbone networks in remote sensing, we devise a self-supervised approach for pre-training deep neural networks. By exploiting the correspondence between geo-tagged audio recordings and remote sensing imagery, this is done in a completely label-free manner, eliminating the need for laborious manual annotation. For this purpose, we introduce the SoundingEarth dataset, which consists of co-located aerial imagery and audio samples all around the world. Using this dataset, we then pre-train ResNet models to map samples from both modalities into a common embedding space, which encourages the models to understand key properties of a scene that influence both visual and auditory appearance. To validate the usefulness of the proposed approach, we evaluate the transfer learning performance of pre-trained weights obtained against weights obtained through other means. By fine-tuning the models on a number of commonly used remote sensing datasets, we show that our approach outperforms existing pre-training strategies for remote sensing imagery. The dataset, code and pre-trained model weights will be available at https://github.com/khdlr/SoundingEarth.
key_contribution: The paper introduces SoundingEarth, a large-scale dataset of 50K+ co-located aerial image and audio pairs, and proposes a self-supervised audiovisual pretraining framework for remote sensing. By aligning image and audio embeddings with a batch triplet loss, the learned visual backbones outperform common initialization and prior self-supervised baselines on multiple remote sensing downstream tasks.

pretraining.objective: image-audio embedding alignment with Batch Triplet Loss

affiliations: Deutsches Zentrum für Luft- und Raumfahrt e. V. (DLR)

title: SpectralGPT: Spectral Remote Sensing Foundation Model
venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
tldr: The paper introduces SpectralGPT, described as the first remote sensing foundation model purpose-built for spectral data, using a 3D MAE-like generative transformer that explicitly models spatial-spectral coupling and spectral sequentiality. Trained progressively on over one million Sentinel-2 spectral images, it achieves strong gains across scene classification, semantic segmentation, and change detection.
abstract: The foundation model has recently garnered significant attention due to its potential to revolutionize the field of visual representation learning in a self-supervised manner. While most foundation models are tailored to effectively process RGB images for various visual tasks, there is a noticeable gap in research focused on spectral data, which offers valuable information for scene understanding, especially in remote sensing (RS) applications. To fill this gap, we created for the first time a universal RS foundation model, named SpectralGPT, which is purpose-built to handle spectral RS images using a novel 3D generative pretrained transformer (GPT). Compared to existing foundation models, SpectralGPT 1) accommodates input images with varying sizes, resolutions, time series, and regions in a progressive training fashion, enabling full utilization of extensive RS big data; 2) leverages 3D token generation for spatial-spectral coupling; 3) captures spectrally sequential patterns via multi-target reconstruction; 4) trains on one million spectral RS images, yielding models with over 600 million parameters. Our evaluation highlights significant performance improvements with pretrained SpectralGPT models, signifying substantial potential in advancing spectral RS big data applications within the field of geoscience across four downstream tasks: single/multi-label scene classification, semantic segmentation, and change detection.
key_contribution: The paper introduces SpectralGPT, described as the first remote sensing foundation model purpose-built for spectral data, using a 3D MAE-like generative transformer that explicitly models spatial-spectral coupling and spectral sequentiality. Trained progressively on over one million Sentinel-2 spectral images, it achieves strong gains across scene classification, semantic segmentation, and change detection.

pretraining.objective: masked image reconstruction with token-to-token and spectral-to-spectral reconstruction losses

pretraining_data.num_images: about 1.47M total available; pretraining uses 712,874 fMoW-S2 images and 354,196 BigEarthNet-S2 images
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: global for fMoW-S2 and Europe for BigEarthNet-S2

title: Geographical Knowledge-driven Representation Learning for Remote Sensing Images
venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
tldr: Proposes GeoKR, a novel pre-training paradigm for remote sensing that uses global land cover products (GlobeLand30) and image geolocation as weak geographical-knowledge supervision instead of manual labels. Introduces a mean-teacher framework to suppress noise from time/resolution discrepancies between images and land cover maps, and constructs Levir-KR, a 1.43M image multi-resolution Gaofen pre-training dataset. Demonstrates improvements over ImageNet pre-training and self-supervised baselines (MoCo/SimCLR/BYOL) on multiple downstream RS tasks.
key_contribution: Proposes GeoKR, a novel pre-training paradigm for remote sensing that uses global land cover products (GlobeLand30) and image geolocation as weak geographical-knowledge supervision instead of manual labels. Introduces a mean-teacher framework to suppress noise from time/resolution discrepancies between images and land cover maps, and constructs Levir-KR, a 1.43M image multi-resolution Gaofen pre-training dataset. Demonstrates improvements over ImageNet pre-training and self-supervised baselines (MoCo/SimCLR/BYOL) on multiple downstream RS tasks.

pretraining.objective: KL divergence between predicted distribution and land-cover knowledge representation, plus KL between student and teacher outputs

pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: global (Gaofen series acquisitions, GlobeLand30 global land cover)

title: An Empirical Study of Remote Sensing Pretraining
venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
tldr: First systematic empirical study of supervised remote sensing pretraining on the large-scale MillionAID dataset across CNN (ResNet-50), transformer (Swin-T), and hybrid (ViTAEv2-S) backbones, demonstrating RSP outperforms ImageNet pretraining on aerial scene tasks and that ViTAEv2-S achieves the best results across recognition, segmentation, detection, and change detection.
key_contribution: First systematic empirical study of supervised remote sensing pretraining on the large-scale MillionAID dataset across CNN (ResNet-50), transformer (Swin-T), and hybrid (ViTAEv2-S) backbones, demonstrating RSP outperforms ImageNet pretraining on aerial scene tasks and that ViTAEv2-S achieves the best results across recognition, segmentation, detection, and change detection.

pretraining.objective: Scene classification (cross-entropy)

title: Advancing Plain Vision Transformer Towards Remote Sensing Foundation Model
venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
tldr: First work to pretrain plain ViT (~100M params) as an RS foundation model via MAE on MillionAID, and proposes Rotated Varied-Size Attention (RVSA) which adds a learnable rotation mechanism to varied-size window attention to better handle arbitrarily oriented objects in remote sensing imagery, achieving SOTA 81.24% mAP on DOTA-V1.0.
key_contribution: First work to pretrain plain ViT (~100M params) as an RS foundation model via MAE on MillionAID, and proposes Rotated Varied-Size Attention (RVSA) which adds a learnable rotation mechanism to varied-size window attention to better handle arbitrarily oriented objects in remote sensing imagery, achieving SOTA 81.24% mAP on DOTA-V1.0.

architecture.backbone: Plain ViT-B and ViTAE-B with Rotated Varied-Size Attention (RVSA)

pretraining.objective: Masked image reconstruction (pixel reconstruction in normalized pixel space)

title: Consecutive Pre-Training: A Knowledge Transfer Learning Strategy with Relevant Unlabeled Data for Remote Sensing Domain
venue: Remote Sensing
tldr: Proposes ConSecutive PreTraining (CSPT), a knowledge transfer learning strategy that does not stop pretraining: first MIM-based self-supervised pretraining on large-scale natural scene data (ImageNet), then continued self-supervised pretraining on relevant unlabeled remote sensing data to gradually bridge the domain gap, mitigate task-aware discrepancy, and unlock the potential of unlabeled RS data without expensive labeling.
key_contribution: Proposes ConSecutive PreTraining (CSPT), a knowledge transfer learning strategy that does not stop pretraining: first MIM-based self-supervised pretraining on large-scale natural scene data (ImageNet), then continued self-supervised pretraining on relevant unlabeled remote sensing data to gradually bridge the domain gap, mitigate task-aware discrepancy, and unlock the potential of unlabeled RS data without expensive labeling.

pretraining.objective: Masked image reconstruction (L1 pixel loss on masked patches)

pretraining_data.num_images: 1.28M (ImageNet-1K) + remote sensing data for further pretraining

title: Towards Geospatial Foundation Models via Continual Pretraining
venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
tldr: Proposes a multi-objective continual pretraining paradigm that leverages ImageNet-22k representations as a teacher (via feature distillation) combined with masked image modeling on a curated diverse remote sensing dataset (GeoPile, ~600k images). Achieves SOTA across 7 geospatial downstream tasks while requiring substantially less compute and carbon footprint (8x reduction vs SatMAE).
key_contribution: Proposes a multi-objective continual pretraining paradigm that leverages ImageNet-22k representations as a teacher (via feature distillation) combined with masked image modeling on a curated diverse remote sensing dataset (GeoPile, ~600k images). Achieves SOTA across 7 geospatial downstream tasks while requiring substantially less compute and carbon footprint (8x reduction vs SatMAE).

pretraining.objective: Masked image modeling (L1 pixel reconstruction) + feature distillation (cosine similarity to teacher features)

title: SwiMDiff: Scene-wide Matching Contrastive Learning with Diffusion Constraint for Remote Sensing Image
venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
tldr: First framework to integrate a diffusion model as an auxiliary pixel-level constraint with contrastive learning for RS SSL, combined with a scene-wide matching strategy that adaptively relabels false negatives from the same geographic scene to improve both global semantic and fine-grained representation learning.
key_contribution: First framework to integrate a diffusion model as an auxiliary pixel-level constraint with contrastive learning for RS SSL, combined with a scene-wide matching strategy that adaptively relabels false negatives from the same geographic scene to improve both global semantic and fine-grained representation learning.

pretraining.objective: Joint loss L = lambda_C * L_C (scene-wide matching contrastive loss) + lambda_D * L_D (diffusion noise prediction L2 loss), with lambda_C=1 and lambda_D=10

pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: global (Sen12MS subset, 256x256 tiles sampled from each season)

title: Predicting Gradient is Better: Exploring Self-Supervised Learning for SAR ATR with a Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture
venue: Isprs Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
tldr: First joint-embedding predictive architecture for SAR ATR that uses multi-scale SAR gradient-by-ratio features as high-quality target signals to suppress speckle noise, combined with local masking (LoMaR) for small targets in remote sensing; demonstrates SSL scalability for building a SAR foundation model unified across targets, scenes, and sensors.
key_contribution: First joint-embedding predictive architecture for SAR ATR that uses multi-scale SAR gradient-by-ratio features as high-quality target signals to suppress speckle noise, combined with local masking (LoMaR) for small targets in remote sensing; demonstrates SSL scalability for building a SAR foundation model unified across targets, scenes, and sensors.

pretraining.method: Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture (JEPA) with Masked Image Modeling
pretraining.objective: MSE between predicted DNN features and multi-scale SAR gradient features for masked local patches

pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Global / multiple scenes (ground, ocean, airports, harbors, nearshore, islands, distant seas, urban) plus simulated

downstream_tasks: Few-shot fine-grained SAR target classification (vehicle, ship, aircraft)

title: SkySense: A Multi-Modal Remote Sensing Foundation Model Towards Universal Interpretation for Earth Observation Imagery
venue: Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
tldr: Largest (2.06B params) multi-modal remote sensing foundation model to date, featuring a factorized multi-modal spatiotemporal encoder, Multi-Granularity Contrastive Learning across pixel/object/image levels, and unsupervised Geo-Context Prototype Learning for region-aware representations. Achieves SOTA on 16 datasets across 7 tasks, outperforming 18 prior RSFMs.
key_contribution: Largest (2.06B params) multi-modal remote sensing foundation model to date, featuring a factorized multi-modal spatiotemporal encoder, Multi-Granularity Contrastive Learning across pixel/object/image levels, and unsupervised Geo-Context Prototype Learning for region-aware representations. Achieves SOTA on 16 datasets across 7 tasks, outperforming 18 prior RSFMs.

architecture.backbone: Swin-H (HSROI) + ViT-L (TMsI) + ViT-L (TSARI) spatial encoders with Multi-Modal Temporal Fusion Transformer
architecture.type: Hybrid (Swin Transformer + ViT) factorized multi-modal spatiotemporal encoder

pretraining.method: Multi-Granularity Contrastive Learning (MGCL) + Cross-Modal Alignment (CMA) + Unsupervised Geo-Context Prototype Learning (GCPL)
pretraining.objective: Combined loss L = alpha * L_MGCL + beta * L_align (multi-granularity contrastive + cross-modal alignment), with geo-context prototype learning incorporated implicitly via EMA updates

datasets: Curated multi-modal RSI dataset (HSROIs from WorldView-3, TMsI from Sentinel-2, TSARI from Sentinel-1)
pretraining_data.num_images: 21.5 million temporal sequences (each sample: 1 static HSROI + 20 Sentinel-2 multispectral images + 10 Sentinel-1 SAR images)
modalities: Multispectral optical time series (TMsI; B2-8, B8A, B11-12)
SAR time series (TSARI; VV, VH polarization)
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: High-resolution (WorldView-3, sub-meter) for HSROI; ~10m for Sentinel-2 TMsI; ~10m for Sentinel-1 TSARI
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Global; globe partitioned into 4096 regions covering diverse scenarios across resolution, spectrum, and imaging mechanism

title: Semantic-aware Dense Representation Learning for Remote Sensing Image Change Detection
venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
tldr: Proposes SaDL, a semantic-aware dense self-supervised pre-training method for remote sensing change detection that combines class-balanced point sampling with semantic masks, point-level cross-view consistency, semantic dissimilarity loss, and a background-swap third view, yielding spatially-sensitive and foreground-discriminative features that significantly improve data efficiency on LEVIR-CD, WHU-CD, and Guangzhou-CD.
key_contribution: Proposes SaDL, a semantic-aware dense self-supervised pre-training method for remote sensing change detection that combines class-balanced point sampling with semantic masks, point-level cross-view consistency, semantic dissimilarity loss, and a background-swap third view, yielding spatially-sensitive and foreground-discriminative features that significantly improve data efficiency on LEVIR-CD, WHU-CD, and Guangzhou-CD.

pretraining.objective: Combined loss: semantic dissimilar loss (L_sd) + cross-view similarity losses (L_s1 between view 1/2, L_s2 between view 1/3 for foreground)

pretraining_data.num_images: ~45k 256x256 patches cut from 180 images of size 5000x5000 (building/non-building masks)
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Cities covered by Inria dataset (e.g., multiple urban regions across US and Europe)

title: HyperSIGMA: Hyperspectral Intelligence Comprehension Foundation Model
venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
tldr: First billion-parameter foundation model specifically designed for hyperspectral image (HSI) interpretation, introducing Sparse Sampling Attention (SSA) and Spectral Enhancement Module (SEM) within a dual spatial-spectral ViT architecture, pre-trained on the newly constructed HyperGlobal-450K dataset (largest global HSI dataset). Provides a unified solution for both high-level and low-level hyperspectral tasks.
key_contribution: First billion-parameter foundation model specifically designed for hyperspectral image (HSI) interpretation, introducing Sparse Sampling Attention (SSA) and Spectral Enhancement Module (SEM) within a dual spatial-spectral ViT architecture, pre-trained on the newly constructed HyperGlobal-450K dataset (largest global HSI dataset). Provides a unified solution for both high-level and low-level hyperspectral tasks.

architecture.backbone: ViT (Vision Transformer) with dual subnetworks (SpatViT spatial + SpecViT spectral) using Sparse Sampling Attention (SSA) and Spectral Enhancement Module (SEM)

pretraining.objective: Reconstruction of masked patches/spectral tokens

pretraining_data.num_images: ~450K hyperspectral images (447,072 HSI patches: 247,072 EO-1 + 200,000 GF-5), equivalent to over 20 million trispectral images
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Global (EO-1 acquired 2011-2017 worldwide; GF-5 covering China)

title: RingMoE: Mixture-of-Modality-Experts Multi-Modal Foundation Models for Universal Remote Sensing Image Interpretation
venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
tldr: First sparse Mixture-of-Experts foundation model for remote sensing, scaled to 14.7B parameters across four modalities (Opt, MS, SAR-L1, SAR-L2). Introduces modal-specialized, collaborative, and shared experts (RMoE), physics-informed SSL with power reconstruction for complex-valued SAR, and dynamic expert pruning for efficient deployment (down to 1B). Achieves 23 SOTAs across 25 benchmarks on six RS tasks.
key_contribution: First sparse Mixture-of-Experts foundation model for remote sensing, scaled to 14.7B parameters across four modalities (Opt, MS, SAR-L1, SAR-L2). Introduces modal-specialized, collaborative, and shared experts (RMoE), physics-informed SSL with power reconstruction for complex-valued SAR, and dynamic expert pruning for efficient deployment (down to 1B). Achieves 23 SOTAs across 25 benchmarks on six RS tasks.

pretraining.method: Masked Image Modeling (MIM) with physics-informed self-supervised learning
pretraining.objective: Masked reconstruction loss (pixel reconstruction + power reconstruction for SAR-L1) combined with expert load-balance loss

title: RingMo-Agent: A Unified Remote Sensing Foundation Model for Multi-Platform and Multi-Modal Reasoning
venue: arXiv.org
tldr: First unified RS vision-language foundation model handling multi-platform (satellite + UAV) and multi-modal (optical, SAR, IR) data across 8 perception and reasoning tasks, supported by the new 3M-pair RS-VL3M dataset, modality-specific embedding layers to mitigate cross-modal interference, and a token-based high-dimensional hidden state trajectory decoder enabling long-horizon spatial reasoning such as aerial navigation.
key_contribution: First unified RS vision-language foundation model handling multi-platform (satellite + UAV) and multi-modal (optical, SAR, IR) data across 8 perception and reasoning tasks, supported by the new 3M-pair RS-VL3M dataset, modality-specific embedding layers to mitigate cross-modal interference, and a token-based high-dimensional hidden state trajectory decoder enabling long-horizon spatial reasoning such as aerial navigation.

architecture.backbone: SigLIP-SO400M-384 visual encoder + DeepSeekMoE (3B) LLM with MLP projector and trajectory decoder

pretraining.method: Two-stage training: vision-language generation stage then instruction-tuning stage
pretraining.objective: Generative language modeling (cross-entropy) + MSE trajectory regression

pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: variable; images dynamically tiled to multiples of 384
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: global; satellite and UAV platforms over diverse urban and natural scenes

title: RS-DFM: A Remote Sensing Distributed Foundation Model for Diverse Downstream Tasks
venue: arXiv.org
tldr: A distributed foundation model for multi-platform (multi-UAV) collaborative remote sensing perception, introducing a Generalized BEV Generation module that uses geometric priors with relative depth estimation to unify features across altitudes/perspectives, and a High-Low Frequency Decoupled Collaboration module for bandwidth-efficient inter-platform feature transmission, along with the AirCo-MultiTasks simulation dataset.
key_contribution: A distributed foundation model for multi-platform (multi-UAV) collaborative remote sensing perception, introducing a Generalized BEV Generation module that uses geometric priors with relative depth estimation to unify features across altitudes/perspectives, and a High-Low Frequency Decoupled Collaboration module for bandwidth-efficient inter-platform feature transmission, along with the AirCo-MultiTasks simulation dataset.

pretraining.objective: Multi-task collaborative perception loss across detection, segmentation, and trajectory prediction

pretraining_data.num_images: 38,000 synchronous images (170 training scenes, 30 validation scenes)
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: 1600x900 pixels per image; collaborative region ~150m x 150m
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: CARLA simulated urban scenes (33 locations from high-quality simulation maps)

title: HSIGene: A Foundation Model for Hyperspectral Image Generation
venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
tldr: First and largest HSI foundation model for hyperspectral image generation based on latent diffusion, supporting multi-condition composable control (HED, segmentation, sketch, MLSD, content, text). Introduces a novel two-stage super-resolution data augmentation framework (DSRNet + RGAN with rectangular guided attention) to alleviate HSI scarcity while preserving spectral fidelity, enabling generation of high-quality synthetic HSIs that improve downstream denoising and super-resolution performance.
key_contribution: First and largest HSI foundation model for hyperspectral image generation based on latent diffusion, supporting multi-condition composable control (HED, segmentation, sketch, MLSD, content, text). Introduces a novel two-stage super-resolution data augmentation framework (DSRNet + RGAN with rectangular guided attention) to alleviate HSI scarcity while preserving spectral fidelity, enabling generation of high-quality synthetic HSIs that improve downstream denoising and super-resolution performance.

architecture.backbone: Latent Diffusion Model (UNet) with ControlNet encoder and VAE

pretraining.method: Latent Diffusion (DDPM training with DDIM sampling)
pretraining.objective: Noise prediction (MSE between predicted and added Gaussian noise) in latent space for controllable HSI generation

pretraining_data.num_images: ~7k training patches (256x256, stride 128) plus ~40k augmented patches via super-resolution; ~55k high-resolution RGB images from Google Earth Engine for DSRNet training
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: 0.5m to 2.5m GSD (Xiongan 0.5m, Chikusei 2.5m, DFC2013 2.5m, DFC2018 1m, Heihe 1m)
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: China (Xiongan, Heihe), Japan (Chikusei), and DFC2013/DFC2018 benchmark scenes; spectral range 380-1018nm aligned to 400-1000nm with 48 bands

title: RS-vHeat: Heat Conduction Guided Efficient Remote Sensing Foundation Model
venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
tldr: First remote sensing foundation model based on heat conduction theory, using a Heat Conduction Operator with O(N^1.5) complexity and global receptive field; combined with frequency-domain hierarchical masking and multi-domain reconstruction self-supervised pretraining on multi-modal optical+SAR data, achieving 84% memory reduction, 24% FLOPs reduction, and 2.7x throughput improvement over attention-based RSFMs while matching or exceeding SOTA accuracy across 4 tasks and 10 datasets
key_contribution: First remote sensing foundation model based on heat conduction theory, using a Heat Conduction Operator with O(N^1.5) complexity and global receptive field; combined with frequency-domain hierarchical masking and multi-domain reconstruction self-supervised pretraining on multi-modal optical+SAR data, achieving 84% memory reduction, 24% FLOPs reduction, and 2.7x throughput improvement over attention-based RSFMs while matching or exceeding SOTA accuracy across 4 tasks and 10 datasets

architecture.backbone: vHeat-B with Heat Conduction Operator (HCO) and Spatial Correction Embeddings (SCEs)
architecture.type: Heat-conduction-based visual encoder (Swin-B-style 4-stage hierarchy with 2,2,18,2 blocks)

pretraining.method: Self-supervised frequency domain hierarchical masking with multi-domain reconstruction and contrastive learning
pretraining.objective: Combined loss: spatial domain reconstruction (L1) + frequency domain reconstruction (L1) + contrastive loss (L_total = L_Con + L_Spa + L_Fre)

datasets: RingMo-style multi-modal dataset (450k matched optical-SAR pairs, ~3M total entries)

title: Can Generative Geospatial Diffusion Models Excel as Discriminative Geospatial Foundation Models?
venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
tldr: First comprehensive adaptation of a large-scale diffusion-based generative model as a self-supervised geospatial foundation model for discriminative remote sensing tasks, introducing three multi-stage feature fusion strategies (global weighted, localized weighted, and mixture-of-experts) that enable diffusion features to rival or exceed state-of-the-art GFMs on GEO-Bench (gains up to +5.7% mIoU in segmentation and +7.9% F1 in classification).
key_contribution: First comprehensive adaptation of a large-scale diffusion-based generative model as a self-supervised geospatial foundation model for discriminative remote sensing tasks, introducing three multi-stage feature fusion strategies (global weighted, localized weighted, and mixture-of-experts) that enable diffusion features to rival or exceed state-of-the-art GFMs on GEO-Bench (gains up to +5.7% mIoU in segmentation and +7.9% F1 in classification).

architecture.backbone: DiffusionSat (Stable Diffusion v2-1 based Latent Diffusion Model with U-Net)
architecture.type: Latent Diffusion Model (U-Net with ResNet, self-attention, and cross-attention blocks)

pretraining.method: Diffusion-based generative self-supervised learning (repurposed as SSL feature extractor)
pretraining.objective: Denoising diffusion (noise prediction) objective inherited from DiffusionSat / Stable Diffusion v2-1

datasets: Stable Diffusion v2-1 web-scraped data (for SD v2-1 backbone variant)
sensors: multi-spectral satellite (RGB bands used in SatDiFuser experiments)

title: CMID: A Unified Self-Supervised Learning Framework for Remote Sensing Image Understanding
venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
tldr: First unified SSL framework for remote sensing that combines contrastive learning (CL) with masked image modeling (MIM) via teacher-student self-distillation, learning representations that are simultaneously globally semantic separable and locally spatially perceptible. Architecture-agnostic (works with both CNNs and ViTs), enabling strong transfer across classification, segmentation, detection, and change detection.
key_contribution: First unified SSL framework for remote sensing that combines contrastive learning (CL) with masked image modeling (MIM) via teacher-student self-distillation, learning representations that are simultaneously globally semantic separable and locally spatially perceptible. Architecture-agnostic (works with both CNNs and ViTs), enabling strong transfer across classification, segmentation, detection, and change detection.

pretraining.objective: L_spat (L1 spatial reconstruction) + L_freq (focal frequency loss) + L_NCE (global contrastive InfoNCE) + L_local (local prototype assignment consistency)

title: Remote Sensing Vision-Language Foundation Models without Annotations via Ground Remote Alignment
venue: International Conference on Learning Representations
tldr: First large-scale vision-language foundation model for remote sensing trained without any textual annotations, by using co-located internet ground images as an intermediary between satellite imagery and language; achieves SOTA zero-shot classification, retrieval, segmentation, and VQA on satellite imagery, outperforming supervised VLMs by up to 20% on classification and 80% on segmentation.
key_contribution: First large-scale vision-language foundation model for remote sensing trained without any textual annotations, by using co-located internet ground images as an intermediary between satellite imagery and language; achieves SOTA zero-shot classification, retrieval, segmentation, and VQA on satellite imagery, outperforming supervised VLMs by up to 20% on classification and 80% on segmentation.

pretraining.method: Ground Remote Alignment for Training (GRAFT) - contrastive alignment between satellite images and co-located internet ground images via frozen CLIP encoder
pretraining.objective: Multi-positive contrastive loss (CLIP-style with multiple positives per satellite image)

pretraining_data.num_images: 10.2 million NAIP pairs and 8.7 million Sentinel-2 pairs

title: DynamicVis: Dynamic Visual Perception for Efficient Remote Sensing Foundation Models
tldr: A remote sensing foundation model explicitly tailored to spatial sparsity of RS imagery via a Dynamic Region-Aware SSM backbone that adaptively routes only task-relevant high-salience tokens through dual-path Mamba scanning while preserving background context via parameter-free integration. Combined with a novel Region-Level Meta-Embedding MIL pre-training paradigm, it processes 2048x2048 imagery with only 97 ms latency and 833 MB memory (~6% and 3% of a base ViT) while achieving SOTA on sparse-target tasks.
key_contribution: A remote sensing foundation model explicitly tailored to spatial sparsity of RS imagery via a Dynamic Region-Aware SSM backbone that adaptively routes only task-relevant high-salience tokens through dual-path Mamba scanning while preserving background context via parameter-free integration. Combined with a novel Region-Level Meta-Embedding MIL pre-training paradigm, it processes 2048x2048 imagery with only 97 ms latency and 833 MB memory (~6% and 3% of a base ViT) while achieving SOTA on sparse-target tasks.

architecture.backbone: Dynamic Region-Aware State Space Model (SSM) with hierarchical multi-scale extractor and Feature Pyramid Network
architecture.type: State Space Model (Mamba-based) with adaptive token routing

pretraining.objective: MIL-NCE contrastive loss + classification cross-entropy

datasets: fMoW (Functional Map of the World) - RGB version

title: AlphaEarth Foundations: An embedding field model for accurate and efficient global mapping from sparse label data
venue: arXiv.org
tldr: First EO featurization model with continuous-time conditioning that fuses multi-source/multi-modal spatial-temporal data via a Space Time Precision (STP) architecture, producing compact 64-byte 10 m global embedding fields. AEF consistently outperforms designed and learned featurization baselines (~23.9% mean error reduction) across 15 sparse-label evaluations and is released as annual global embedding layers (2017-2024) on Google Earth Engine.
key_contribution: First EO featurization model with continuous-time conditioning that fuses multi-source/multi-modal spatial-temporal data via a Space Time Precision (STP) architecture, producing compact 64-byte 10 m global embedding fields. AEF consistently outperforms designed and learned featurization baselines (~23.9% mean error reduction) across 15 sparse-label evaluations and is released as annual global embedding layers (2017-2024) on Google Earth Engine.

architecture.backbone: Space Time Precision (STP) transformer with interleaved space (ViT-like spatial self-attention), time (time-axial self-attention), and precision (3x3 conv) operators; trio of teacher video embedding model with implicit decoders, student video embedding model, and text alignment model
architecture.type: Transformer (custom STP video encoder with implicit decoders)

pretraining.method: Self-supervised contrastive video teacher-student with implicit decoder reconstruction, batch uniformity objective on S^63, and CLIP-style text alignment
pretraining.objective: Implicit decoder reconstruction losses per source + batch uniformity (uniform distribution on S^63) + contrastive text-video alignment (f_CLIP)

pretraining_data.num_images: ~3 billion observations across nine gridded data sources and one unstructured text source, covering ~1.1% of Earth's land surface

downstream_tasks: Change detection (land cover change, land use change)

title: TiMo: Spatiotemporal Foundation Model for Satellite Image Time Series
venue: arXiv.org
tldr: A hierarchical vision transformer foundation model for satellite image time series, introducing Spatiotemporal Gyroscope Attention (STGA) and its differential variant (D-STGA) that compute attention only among tokens sharing the same spatial or temporal coordinate to efficiently capture multiscale spatiotemporal relationships, pretrained via masked image modeling on the new MillionST dataset (1M Sentinel-2 SITS from 100k locations x 10 timestamps).
key_contribution: A hierarchical vision transformer foundation model for satellite image time series, introducing Spatiotemporal Gyroscope Attention (STGA) and its differential variant (D-STGA) that compute attention only among tokens sharing the same spatial or temporal coordinate to efficiently capture multiscale spatiotemporal relationships, pretrained via masked image modeling on the new MillionST dataset (1M Sentinel-2 SITS from 100k locations x 10 timestamps).

architecture.backbone: Hierarchical Vision Transformer (Swin-style, 4 stages) with Differential Spatiotemporal Gyroscope Attention (D-STGA) in early stages and MHSA in deeper stages

pretraining.method: Masked Image Modeling (Hiera-style MAE adapted to hierarchical ViT)
pretraining.objective: Reconstruction of masked spatiotemporal patches

pretraining_data.num_images: ~1,000,000 Sentinel-2 SITS samples from 100,000 geographic locations across 1,317 cities, each with 10 temporal phases over 5 years (2017-2022)
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Global, primarily Europe, North Africa, and West Asia (1,317 cities ranked among the 10,000 most populous globally)

title: CGEarthEye: A High-Resolution Remote Sensing Vision Foundation Model Based on the Jilin-1 Satellite Constellation
venue: arXiv.org
tldr: First high-resolution (sub-meter) remote sensing vision foundation model trained on the Jilin-1 satellite constellation, introducing JLSSD—the first 15-million-scale globally distributed multi-temporal SSL dataset with quarterly sampling—and a multi-granularity SSL framework combining augmentation-aware, seasonal, and masked patch contrastive learning, achieving SOTA across 10 high-resolution benchmarks under frozen-backbone fine-tuning.
key_contribution: First high-resolution (sub-meter) remote sensing vision foundation model trained on the Jilin-1 satellite constellation, introducing JLSSD—the first 15-million-scale globally distributed multi-temporal SSL dataset with quarterly sampling—and a multi-granularity SSL framework combining augmentation-aware, seasonal, and masked patch contrastive learning, achieving SOTA across 10 high-resolution benchmarks under frozen-backbone fine-tuning.

pretraining.objective: L = L_classtoken + L_classtoken_season + L_patch (cross-entropy contrastive losses)

pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Global (8.06M quarterly samples from 2.015M Chinese locations + 7.985M annual mosaic samples from non-Chinese locations, 2023)

title: MAPEX: Modality-Aware Pruning of Experts for Remote Sensing Foundation Models
venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
tldr: Introduces a multi-modal pre-training approach using a mixture-of-modality experts ViT with modality-conditioned token routing, plus a modality-aware expert pruning technique that turns a large multi-modal foundation model into smaller, efficient modality-specific models tailored to downstream tasks.
key_contribution: Introduces a multi-modal pre-training approach using a mixture-of-modality experts ViT with modality-conditioned token routing, plus a modality-aware expert pruning technique that turns a large multi-modal foundation model into smaller, efficient modality-specific models tailored to downstream tasks.

pretraining.method: Masked Image Modeling (MAE) with Mixture-of-Modality Experts
pretraining.objective: Mean-squared error pixel reconstruction of masked patches per modality

title: SkySense V2: A Unified Foundation Model for Multi-modal Remote Sensing
venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
tldr: A unified MM-RSFM that uses a single transformer backbone (665M params) to process HR optical, MS, and SAR modalities simultaneously, with novel Adaptive Patch Merging for resolution harmonization, modality-specific learnable prompt tokens, MoE scaling, and a new Query-based Semantic Aggregation Contrastive Learning (QSACL) SSL strategy tailored to RS images' multi-subject semantic distribution; outperforms SkySense by 1.8 points on average across 16 datasets and 7 tasks.
key_contribution: A unified MM-RSFM that uses a single transformer backbone (665M params) to process HR optical, MS, and SAR modalities simultaneously, with novel Adaptive Patch Merging for resolution harmonization, modality-specific learnable prompt tokens, MoE scaling, and a new Query-based Semantic Aggregation Contrastive Learning (QSACL) SSL strategy tailored to RS images' multi-subject semantic distribution; outperforms SkySense by 1.8 points on average across 16 datasets and 7 tasks.

architecture.backbone: Unified transformer encoder with Swin Transformer V2 Blocks (SwinV2B) in stages 1-2 and vanilla Transformer Blocks in stages 3-4, with Adaptive Patch Merging (APM) modules and Mixture of Experts (MoE)

pretraining.method: Self-supervised multi-modal contrastive learning with teacher-student EMA framework
pretraining.objective: Weighted sum of MGCL, ITA, and QSACL losses

pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: HR optical 2048x2048; Sentinel-2 and Sentinel-1 64x64 (avg 65 S2 images and SAR sequences per set)

title: TESSERA: Temporal Embeddings of Surface Spectra for Earth Representation and Analysis
venue: arXiv.org
tldr: A pixel-wise self-supervised foundation model for multi-modal Sentinel-1/2 time series that learns label-efficient embeddings via temporal sampling invariance, introducing the d-pixel representation, global shuffling, and mix-up regularization; releases FAIR global, annual, 10 m pixel-wise int8 embeddings as analysis-ready 'embeddings-as-data' through the GeoTessera Python library, achieving state-of-the-art accuracy across classification, segmentation, and regression benchmarks with minimal computation
key_contribution: A pixel-wise self-supervised foundation model for multi-modal Sentinel-1/2 time series that learns label-efficient embeddings via temporal sampling invariance, introducing the d-pixel representation, global shuffling, and mix-up regularization; releases FAIR global, annual, 10 m pixel-wise int8 embeddings as analysis-ready 'embeddings-as-data' through the GeoTessera Python library, achieving state-of-the-art accuracy across classification, segmentation, and regression benchmarks with minimal computation

title: CRS-Diff: Controllable Remote Sensing Image Generation with Diffusion Model
venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
tldr: First multiple-condition controllable diffusion-based generative model for remote sensing imagery, supporting simultaneous text, metadata, and image (HED, MLSD, depth, sketch, segmentation, roadmap, content) conditions via a multi-scale Attention Feature Fusion mechanism over a ControlNet-augmented Stable Diffusion backbone.
key_contribution: First multiple-condition controllable diffusion-based generative model for remote sensing imagery, supporting simultaneous text, metadata, and image (HED, MLSD, depth, sketch, segmentation, roadmap, content) conditions via a multi-scale Attention Feature Fusion mechanism over a ControlNet-augmented Stable Diffusion backbone.

pretraining.objective: Denoising score matching (noise prediction MSE) with classifier-free guidance

modalities: metadata (year, month, day, GSD, latitude, longitude, cloud cover)
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: 224x224 (RSICD, fMoW) and varying (Million-AID); resized to 512x512 for training

downstream_tasks: data augmentation for downstream tasks (e.g., road extraction)

title: SenPa-MAE: Sensor Parameter Aware Masked Autoencoder for Multi-Satellite Self-Supervised Pretraining
venue: German Conference on Pattern Recognition
tldr: Introduces a sensor-parameter-aware MAE that injects per-channel spectral response functions and GSD into ViT embeddings via a versatile encoding module, plus a Spectral Superposition Augmentation strategy, enabling multi-satellite self-supervised pretraining and sensor-independent inference across Sentinel-2, Planet SuperDove and Landsat.
key_contribution: Introduces a sensor-parameter-aware MAE that injects per-channel spectral response functions and GSD into ViT embeddings via a versatile encoding module, plus a Spectral Superposition Augmentation strategy, enabling multi-satellite self-supervised pretraining and sensor-independent inference across Sentinel-2, Planet SuperDove and Landsat.

architecture.backbone: ViT-Base (12 encoder layers, 12 heads; 3 decoder layers, 12 heads)

pretraining.method: Masked Autoencoder (MAE) with Sensor Parameter Encoding
pretraining.objective: Masked mean absolute error reconstruction of unmasked input

datasets: Custom multi-sensor dataset (Sentinel-2, Planet SuperDove, Landsat-8/9)
pretraining_data.num_images: ~116k patches of 256x256 px from 26 training locations (50k S2, 50k SD, 25k LS)
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: ~3.5 m (SD), 10 m and 20 m (S2), 30 m (LS); resampled to 5 m pixel spacing

title: One for All: Toward Unified Foundation Models for Earth Vision
venue: IEEE International Geoscience and Remote Sensing Symposium
tldr: A unified foundation model using a single shared Transformer backbone with modality-specific patch embeddings to handle multiple remote sensing modalities and spatial resolutions simultaneously, pre-trained via masked image modeling without requiring spatially aligned multi-modal data.
key_contribution: A unified foundation model using a single shared Transformer backbone with modality-specific patch embeddings to handle multiple remote sensing modalities and spatial resolutions simultaneously, pre-trained via masked image modeling without requiring spatially aligned multi-modal data.

pretraining.objective: Masked patch reconstruction

pretraining_data.num_images: ~8.08M total curated (4,642,353 S1 + 977,774 S2 + 117,450 Gaofen + 2,332,351 NAIP + 11,483 EnMAP); 50,000 used for pretraining

title: LeMeViT: Efficient Vision Transformer with Learnable Meta Tokens for Remote Sensing Image Interpretation
venue: International Joint Conference on Artificial Intelligence
tldr: Proposes a hardware-friendly hierarchical Vision Transformer using a small number of learnable meta tokens and a Dual Cross-Attention (DCA) block to reduce self-attention complexity from quadratic to linear, achieving ~1.7x speedup with competitive accuracy on remote sensing tasks.
key_contribution: Proposes a hardware-friendly hierarchical Vision Transformer using a small number of learnable meta tokens and a Dual Cross-Attention (DCA) block to reduce self-attention complexity from quadratic to linear, achieving ~1.7x speedup with competitive accuracy on remote sensing tasks.

pretraining.objective: Supervised classification

title: SMARTIES: Spectrum-Aware Multi-Sensor Auto-Encoder for Remote Sensing Images
venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
tldr: A single sensor-agnostic foundation model that projects heterogeneous RS sensors into a shared spectrum-aware space, enabling unified pretraining across diverse sensors, scalable training without sensor-specific backbones, and generalization to unseen sensors via projection-layer interpolation.
abstract: From optical sensors to microwave radars, leveraging the complementary strengths of remote sensing (RS) sensors is crucial for achieving dense spatio-temporal monitoring of our planet. In contrast, recent deep learning models, whether task-specific or foundational, are often specific to single sensors or to fixed combinations: adapting such models to different sensory inputs requires both architectural changes and re-training, limiting scalability and generalization across multiple RS sensors. On the contrary, a single model able to modulate its feature representations to accept diverse sensors as input would pave the way to agile and flexible multi-sensor RS data processing. To address this, we introduce SMARTIES, a generic and versatile foundation model lifting sensor-specific/dependent efforts and enabling scalability and generalization to diverse RS sensors: SMARTIES projects data from heterogeneous sensors into a shared spectrum-aware space, enabling the use of arbitrary combinations of bands both for training and inference. To obtain sensor-agnostic representations, we train a single, unified transformer model reconstructing masked multi-sensor data with cross-sensor token mixup. On both single- and multi-modal tasks across diverse sensors, SMARTIES outperforms previous models that rely on sensor-specific pretraining. Our code and pretrained models are available at https://gsumbul.github.io/SMARTIES.
key_contribution: A single sensor-agnostic foundation model that projects heterogeneous RS sensors into a shared spectrum-aware space, enabling unified pretraining across diverse sensors, scalable training without sensor-specific backbones, and generalization to unseen sensors via projection-layer interpolation.

pretraining.method: Masked Image Modeling with cross-sensor token mixup
pretraining.objective: Masked patch reconstruction (MSE) in spectrum-aware space

title: TerraFM: A Scalable Foundation Model for Unified Multisensor Earth Observation
venue: arXiv.org
tldr: A scalable multisensor EO foundation model that unifies Sentinel-1 SAR and Sentinel-2 L1C/L2A optical inputs via modality-specific patch embeddings and cross-attention fusion, treats sensing modalities as natural augmentations within a DINO-style contrastive teacher-student framework, and introduces a dual-centering mechanism with class-frequency-aware regularization (using WorldCover priors) to mitigate long-tailed LULC distributions, achieving SoTA on GEO-Bench and Copernicus-Bench.
key_contribution: A scalable multisensor EO foundation model that unifies Sentinel-1 SAR and Sentinel-2 L1C/L2A optical inputs via modality-specific patch embeddings and cross-attention fusion, treats sensing modalities as natural augmentations within a DINO-style contrastive teacher-student framework, and introduces a dual-centering mechanism with class-frequency-aware regularization (using WorldCover priors) to mitigate long-tailed LULC distributions, achieving SoTA on GEO-Bench and Copernicus-Bench.

pretraining.method: Self-supervised contrastive learning (DINO-style student-teacher with multi-crop, modality augmentation, cross-attention fusion, dual centering)
pretraining.objective: Cross-entropy consistency between teacher and student softmax outputs across views (contrastive self-distillation)

datasets: Major-TOM (filtered subset enriched with ESA WorldCover, Köppen-Geiger climate zones, ESRI World Regions metadata)
pretraining_data.num_images: 18.7M modality-specific training tiles (from 1.5M curated 10.68km x 10.68km grid cells, each split into 534x534 tiles)
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Globally distributed terrestrial regions sampled with land-cover, climate-zone, and world-region priors (98% of ocean tiles removed)

title: Harnessing Massive Satellite Imagery with Efficient Masked Image Modeling
venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
tldr: Introduces (1) OpticalRS-13M, a large-scale (13M images) curated optical RS pre-training dataset 4x larger than prior RS datasets, and (2) SelectiveMAE, an efficient MIM method with Progressive Semantic Token Selection using HOG features that achieves >2x pre-training speedup over MAE while improving downstream RS task performance.
abstract: Masked Image Modeling (MIM) has become an essential method for building foundational visual models in remote sensing (RS). However, the limitations in size and diversity of existing RS datasets restrict the ability of MIM methods to learn generalizable representations. Additionally, conventional MIM techniques, which require reconstructing all tokens, introduce unnecessary computational overhead. To address these issues, we present a new pre-training pipeline for RS models, featuring the creation of a large-scale RS dataset and an efficient MIM approach. We curated a high-quality dataset named OpticalRS-13M by collecting publicly available RS datasets and processing them through exclusion, slicing, and deduplication. OpticalRS-13M comprises 13 million optical images covering various RS tasks, such as object detection and pixel segmentation. To enhance efficiency, we propose SelectiveMAE, a pre-training method that dynamically encodes and reconstructs semantically rich patch tokens, thereby reducing the inefficiencies of traditional MIM models caused by redundant background pixels in RS images. Extensive experiments show that OpticalRS-13M significantly improves classification, detection, and segmentation performance, while SelectiveMAE increases training efficiency over 2$\times$ times. This highlights the effectiveness and scalability of our pipeline in developing RS foundational models. The dataset, source code, and trained models will be released at https://github.com/MiliLab/SelectiveMAE.
key_contribution: Introduces (1) OpticalRS-13M, a large-scale (13M images) curated optical RS pre-training dataset 4x larger than prior RS datasets, and (2) SelectiveMAE, an efficient MIM method with Progressive Semantic Token Selection using HOG features that achieves >2x pre-training speedup over MAE while improving downstream RS task performance.

pretraining.objective: Pixel reconstruction (MSE loss) on selected patch tokens

pretraining_data.num_images: 13.2M (OpticalRS-13M); also OpticalRS-4M lightweight version with 3.92M images
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: high-resolution visible light imagery (avg ~199,214 pixels per image in OpticalRS-13M)
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Diverse global coverage curated from multiple publicly available RS datasets via exclusion, slicing, and deduplication; 12 main scene/object/event categories

title: Pattern Integration and Enhancement Vision Transformer for Self-supervised Learning in Remote Sensing
venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
tldr: Introduces PIEViT, a self-supervised ViT for remote sensing that combines a Geospatial Pattern Cohesion (GPC) module to aggregate geospatially clustered patches and a Feature Integration Projection (FIP) module to refine masked token reconstruction, enabling discrimination of internal patch features in scenographic remote sensing imagery and achieving SOTA on object detection, land cover classification, and change detection.
key_contribution: Introduces PIEViT, a self-supervised ViT for remote sensing that combines a Geospatial Pattern Cohesion (GPC) module to aggregate geospatially clustered patches and a Feature Integration Projection (FIP) module to refine masked token reconstruction, enabling discrimination of internal patch features in scenographic remote sensing imagery and achieving SOTA on object detection, land cover classification, and change detection.

pretraining.objective: Combined CLS-level cross-entropy loss and patch-level MIM loss between student and teacher predictions

title: Self-Supervised Spatio-Temporal Representation Learning Of Satellite Image Time Series
venue: IEEE International Geoscience and Remote Sensing Symposium
tldr: A novel Unet+Transformer spatio-temporal architecture (U-BARN) for irregularly sampled, multi-variate Sentinel-2 SITS, combined with a BERT-inspired generative self-supervised pre-training strategy that masks SSE-encoded patch embeddings and reconstructs valid pixels, enabling rich spatio-temporal representations that improve crop and land cover semantic segmentation, especially under label scarcity.
key_contribution: A novel Unet+Transformer spatio-temporal architecture (U-BARN) for irregularly sampled, multi-variate Sentinel-2 SITS, combined with a BERT-inspired generative self-supervised pre-training strategy that masks SSE-encoded patch embeddings and reconstructs valid pixels, enabling rich spatio-temporal representations that improve crop and land cover semantic segmentation, especially under label scarcity.

pretraining.objective: Masked patch reconstruction with MSE loss on valid pixels of corrupted patches

datasets: Sentinel-2 L2A unlabeled dataset over France (13 S2 tiles, 2018-2020)
pretraining_data.num_images: 13 S2 tiles x 10 ROIs of 1024x1024 over 2018-2020 (annual SITS, ~64x64 patch time series used during training)
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: 10 m (20 m bands resampled to 10 m); 4 bands at 10 m + 6 bands at 20 m

title: MAESTRO: Masked AutoEncoders for Multimodal, Multitemporal, and Multispectral Earth Observation Data
venue: IEEE Workshop/Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision
tldr: A tailored MAE adaptation for EO data that orchestrates multimodal, multitemporal, and multispectral inputs via inter-group token-based fusion (early fusion across time steps and similar modalities, late fusion across dissimilar ones) combined with joint-token multispectral fusion using a novel patch-group-wise target normalization that injects a spectral prior at negligible computational cost; achieves SOTA on tasks relying on multitemporal dynamics while remaining competitive on others.
abstract: Urban form plays a crucial role in shaping transportation patterns, accessibility, energy consumption, and more. Our study examines the relationship between urban form and transportation energy use by developing a parametric model that simulates city structures and their impact on travel distances. We explore various urban morphologies, including sprawling, elongated, compact, and vertically concentrated cities, and consider five urban profiles: "needle," "pyramid," "pancake," "bowl," and "ring." We designed an interactive visualisation and calculator that enables the analysis of these effects, providing insights into the impact of various urban configurations. Our model quantifies the average commuting distances associated with these forms, demonstrating that compact and centrally dense cities minimise the total travel distance in cities.
key_contribution: A tailored MAE adaptation for EO data that orchestrates multimodal, multitemporal, and multispectral inputs via inter-group token-based fusion (early fusion across time steps and similar modalities, late fusion across dissimilar ones) combined with joint-token multispectral fusion using a novel patch-group-wise target normalization that injects a spectral prior at negligible computational cost; achieves SOTA on tasks relying on multitemporal dynamics while remaining competitive on others.

pretraining.method: Masked Autoencoding (MAE) with token-based early fusion across time steps and similar modalities, late fusion across dissimilar modalities, and joint-token early fusion for multispectrality with patch-group-wise target normalization
pretraining.objective: Masked patch reconstruction with patch-group-wise normalized targets (L1 loss)

pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: 0.2 m (aerial), 1 m (SPOT 6-7 resampled), 1.25 m (NAIP), 10 m (Sentinel-2), variable (Sentinel-1)
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: France (FLAIR-HUB, FLAIR#2, PASTIS-HD), Germany (TreeSatAI-TS), United States urban areas (S2-NAIP urban)

title: Geography-Aware Self-Supervised Learning
venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
tldr: Introduces geography-aware self-supervised learning that exploits the spatio-temporal structure of remote sensing data via temporal positive pairs (TemporalInfoNCE) and a geo-location classification pretext task, closing the gap between contrastive and supervised learning on remote sensing benchmarks and improving transfer to detection, segmentation, and land cover tasks.
key_contribution: Introduces geography-aware self-supervised learning that exploits the spatio-temporal structure of remote sensing data via temporal positive pairs (TemporalInfoNCE) and a geo-location classification pretext task, closing the gap between contrastive and supervised learning on remote sensing benchmarks and improving transfer to detection, segmentation, and land cover tasks.

pretraining.objective: TemporalInfoNCE contrastive loss + cross-entropy geo-location classification loss

datasets: GeoImageNet (geo-tagged ImageNet subset via FLICKR API)
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: sub-meter to a few meters (fMoW high-resolution satellite)
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Global (seven continents for fMoW; worldwide geo-tagged for GeoImageNet)

title: TEOChat: A Large Vision-Language Assistant for Temporal Earth Observation Data
venue: International Conference on Learning Representations
tldr: First vision-language model for temporal sequences of Earth observation imagery, along with TEOChatlas, the first temporal EO instruction-following dataset; outperforms prior EO VLMs (GeoChat) and video VLMs (Video-LLaVA), rivals specialist models, and surpasses GPT-4o and Gemini-1.5 Pro on multiple temporal EO tasks.
key_contribution: First vision-language model for temporal sequences of Earth observation imagery, along with TEOChatlas, the first temporal EO instruction-following dataset; outperforms prior EO VLMs (GeoChat) and video VLMs (Video-LLaVA), rivals specialist models, and surpasses GPT-4o and Gemini-1.5 Pro on multiple temporal EO tasks.

architecture.backbone: CLIP ViT-L/14 image encoder + 2-layer MLP vision-language connector + LLaMA-2 (Vicuna) LLM decoder

pretraining.objective: Next-token cross-entropy on instruction responses (visual instruction tuning)
