sensors: 8 sensors spanning the EO datasets including Sentinel-2
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: global (considerable geographic diversity across fMoW, xBD, S2Looking, QFabric)

downstream_tasks: change detection (building damage assessment, building change detection)

title: EarthMarker: A Visual Prompting Multi-modal Large Language Model for Remote Sensing
venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
tldr: First visual prompting multi-modal large language model for remote sensing, enabling multi-granularity (image/region/point-level) RS imagery interpretation via box and point visual prompts. Introduces a sharing visual encoding mechanism (visual prompts as images), a cross-domain three-phase training strategy with disjoint parameters, and the RSVP dataset (~3.65M visual prompting instruction pairs) — the first RS visual prompting instruction dataset.
key_contribution: First visual prompting multi-modal large language model for remote sensing, enabling multi-granularity (image/region/point-level) RS imagery interpretation via box and point visual prompts. Introduces a sharing visual encoding mechanism (visual prompts as images), a cross-domain three-phase training strategy with disjoint parameters, and the RSVP dataset (~3.65M visual prompting instruction pairs) — the first RS visual prompting instruction dataset.

architecture.backbone: Mixture of Visual Experts (MoV: DINOv2-ViT-L/14 + CLIP-ConvNeXt-L) + Llama 2 13B LLM decoder
architecture.type: Multi-modal Large Language Model (MLLM) with visual prompting

pretraining.method: Three-phase cross-domain progressive learning: (1) multi-domain image-text alignment, (2) spatial perception tuning, (3) RS visual prompting tuning with LoRA
pretraining.objective: Visual instruction tuning / next-token prediction conditioned on visual prompts and text instructions

pretraining_data.num_images: ~3.65M image-point-text and image-region-text pairings in RSVP
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: Mixed: from 0.05m (Potsdam) to 30m, including VHR (<0.5m) and medium-to-low resolution
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Broad global coverage (most raw data sourced from Google Earth)

title: SkySenseGPT: A Fine-Grained Instruction Tuning Dataset and Model for Remote Sensing Vision-Language Understanding
venue: arXiv.org
tldr: Introduces FIT-RS, a large-scale (1.8M samples) fine-grained instruction tuning dataset for remote sensing built from scene graph annotations, plus the FIT-RSRC benchmark for relation comprehension; proposes SkySenseGPT, an RSLMM that excels at fine-grained relation reasoning and scene graph generation, surpassing prior RSLMMs on public benchmarks.
key_contribution: Introduces FIT-RS, a large-scale (1.8M samples) fine-grained instruction tuning dataset for remote sensing built from scene graph annotations, plus the FIT-RSRC benchmark for relation comprehension; proposes SkySenseGPT, an RSLMM that excels at fine-grained relation reasoning and scene graph generation, surpassing prior RSLMMs on public benchmarks.

architecture.backbone: CLIP-ViT-L/14 (336x336) vision encoder + MLP projector + Vicuna-v1.5 LLM

pretraining.method: Instruction tuning (LoRA fine-tuning of LLM on FIT-RS plus public RS datasets)
pretraining.objective: Autoregressive instruction-following (next-token prediction) for fine-grained remote sensing vision-language understanding

datasets: FIT-RS (built on STAR scene graph dataset)
pretraining_data.num_images: FIT-RS contains 1,800,851 instruction samples derived from 82,532 image patches (cropped from 1,273 large-size VHR RSIs); ~1415k FIT-RS train/val instruction samples + 365k from public datasets used for instruction tuning
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: Very-high-resolution (VHR); large-size RSIs ranging from 512x768 to 27,860x31,096 pixels, cropped via 512x512 sliding window with 100-pixel overlap
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Worldwide VHR remote sensing imagery covering complex semantic scenarios (airports, dams, ports, etc.)

title: SatlasPretrain: A Large-Scale Dataset for Remote Sensing Image Understanding
venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
tldr: A very-large-scale multi-task remote sensing dataset (40x more pixels and 150x more labels than prior datasets) with 137 categories across 7 label types and spatio-temporal image series, plus SatlasNet, a unified multi-head model. Pretraining on SatlasPretrain improves downstream accuracy by 18% over ImageNet and 6% over the next best remote sensing baseline.
key_contribution: A very-large-scale multi-task remote sensing dataset (40x more pixels and 150x more labels than prior datasets) with 137 categories across 7 label types and spatio-temporal image series, plus SatlasNet, a unified multi-head model. Pretraining on SatlasPretrain improves downstream accuracy by 18% over ImageNet and 6% over the next best remote sensing baseline.

pretraining.objective: Multi-task supervised loss (cross-entropy for segmentation/classification, L1 for regression, Faster/Mask R-CNN losses for detection/instance segmentation)

datasets: SatlasPretrain (Sentinel-2 + NAIP imagery, labels from OpenStreetMap, NOAA lidar scans, WorldCover, Microsoft Buildings, C2S, plus expert and AMT annotations)
pretraining_data.num_images: 856K Web-Mercator tiles (zoom 13); 828K train + 28K test low-res tiles with 8-12 Sentinel-2 images each; 46K high-res NAIP tiles (8192x8192) with 3-5 images; 17M image megapixels; 302M labels under 137 categories
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Global for Sentinel-2 (all continents except Antarctica, 21,320,000 sq km covered); United States only for NAIP high-resolution

title: LHRS-Bot-Nova: Improved Multimodal Large Language Model for Remote Sensing Vision-Language Interpretation
venue: arXiv.org
tldr: An RS-specialized MLLM combining a scaled-up SigLIP vision encoder with a novel MoE-based vision perceiver bridge layer, trained on a new feature-guided recaptioned image-caption dataset (LHRS-Align-Recap) and an extended spatial-recognition instruction dataset (LHRS-Instruct-Plus), achieving state-of-the-art performance across RS scene classification, VQA, visual grounding, and the LHRS-Bench MCQ benchmark.
key_contribution: An RS-specialized MLLM combining a scaled-up SigLIP vision encoder with a novel MoE-based vision perceiver bridge layer, trained on a new feature-guided recaptioned image-caption dataset (LHRS-Align-Recap) and an extended spatial-recognition instruction dataset (LHRS-Instruct-Plus), achieving state-of-the-art performance across RS scene classification, VQA, visual grounding, and the LHRS-Bench MCQ benchmark.

architecture.backbone: SigLIP-L/14 vision encoder + LLaMA3-8B LLM with MoE-based vision perceiver bridge

pretraining.method: Three-stage curriculum learning: vision-language pretraining, multi-task instruction fine-tuning, supervised fine-tuning
pretraining.objective: Vision-language alignment via image captioning and instruction-following (autoregressive language modeling)

datasets: LHRS-Align-Recap (feature-guided recaptioned via Share-Captioner using OSM features)
LHRS-Instruct-Plus (generated via GPT-4V from DOTAv2 and FAIR1M)
Multi-task dataset combining RSVQA-HR/LR, UCM, fMoW, METER-ML, RSVG, DIOR-RSVG, RSICD
pretraining_data.num_images: ~1.1M+ (LHRS-Align-Recap caption set; instruction-tuning includes 67K DOTAv2 + 253K FAIR1M conversations + 140K LRV-Instruct + others)
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: global (derived from public RS datasets paired with OSM)

title: A Billion-scale Foundation Model for Remote Sensing Images
venue: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
tldr: First billion-scale (2.4B parameter) foundation model for remote sensing, demonstrating that scaling vision transformers via parallel MHSA/FFN layers (rather than depth) improves performance and sample efficiency on rotated object detection and semantic segmentation downstream tasks.
key_contribution: First billion-scale (2.4B parameter) foundation model for remote sensing, demonstrating that scaling vision transformers via parallel MHSA/FFN layers (rather than depth) improves performance and sample efficiency on rotated object detection and semantic segmentation downstream tasks.

pretraining.objective: Masked image reconstruction (MSE on masked patches)

title: Exploring Masked Autoencoders for Sensor-Agnostic Image Retrieval in Remote Sensing
venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
tldr: First systematic study of masked autoencoders for sensor-agnostic (cross-sensor) content-based image retrieval in remote sensing. Introduces four CSMAE variants exploring sensor-common vs sensor-specific encoders/decoders, three multi-modal masking correspondences, and combined uni-modal + cross-modal reconstruction objectives with optional inter-modal latent similarity preservation, enabling MIM-based representation learning across SAR and multispectral RS modalities.
key_contribution: First systematic study of masked autoencoders for sensor-agnostic (cross-sensor) content-based image retrieval in remote sensing. Introduces four CSMAE variants exploring sensor-common vs sensor-specific encoders/decoders, three multi-modal masking correspondences, and combined uni-modal + cross-modal reconstruction objectives with optional inter-modal latent similarity preservation, enabling MIM-based representation learning across SAR and multispectral RS modalities.

architecture.type: Vision Transformer (multi-sensor encoder + cross-sensor encoder + multi-sensor decoder)

pretraining.objective: Masked image modeling with uni-modal + cross-modal reconstruction (MSE) plus optional inter-modal discrepancy elimination (MDE) or mutual information maximization (MIM)

datasets: BigEarthNet (BEN-270K subset over 10 European countries; BEN-14K subset over Serbia)

title: GeoPixel: Pixel Grounding Large Multimodal Model in Remote Sensing
venue: International Conference on Machine Learning
tldr: First end-to-end high-resolution RS large multimodal model (LMM) supporting pixel-level grounding with multi-target segmentation masks at up to 4K resolution via adaptive image partitioning; introduces GeoPixelD, a semi-automated visually grounded RS dataset for grounded conversation generation, and a benchmark for evaluating RS LMMs on fine-grained visual understanding.
key_contribution: First end-to-end high-resolution RS large multimodal model (LMM) supporting pixel-level grounding with multi-target segmentation masks at up to 4K resolution via adaptive image partitioning; introduces GeoPixelD, a semi-automated visually grounded RS dataset for grounded conversation generation, and a benchmark for evaluating RS LMMs on fine-grained visual understanding.

architecture.backbone: InternLM-XComposer-2.5 (IXC-2.5) 7B LLM (InternLM2 7B) with CLIP ViT-L/14 vision encoder (560x560), SAM-2 (Hiera MAE) grounding vision encoder, and pixel decoder
architecture.type: Vision-Language Multimodal Model with pixel grounding (LMM)

pretraining.method: Instruction tuning / fine-tuning of pretrained LMM with LoRA on grounded conversation generation data
pretraining.objective: Grounded conversation generation (GCG) with multi-target pixel-level segmentation mask prediction and language modeling; <SEG> token embeddings decoded into segmentation masks

pretraining_data.num_images: 16,795 holistic image-level annotations; 36,793 instance-specific annotations; 17,023 group annotations; 600,817 objects; 53,816 grounded phrases
pretraining_data.spatial_resolution: high-resolution aerial imagery cropped to 800x800 patches; supports dynamic input resolution up to 4K
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: derived from iSAID aerial imagery dataset (global aerial scenes)

downstream_datasets: GeoPixelD (RS-GCG benchmark, test set from iSAID validation subset, 5,427 referring expression-segmentation pairs, 61,384 annotated objects)

title: MMEarth: Exploring Multi-Modal Pretext Tasks For Geospatial Representation Learning
venue: European Conference on Computer Vision
tldr: Introduces MMEarth, a global 1.2M-location multi-modal Sentinel-2-aligned pretraining dataset with 12 modalities, and proposes MP-MAE, a ConvNeXt V2-based fully convolutional multi-pretext masked autoencoder that improves fine-tuning and especially linear-probing performance over ImageNet and Sentinel-2-only MAE baselines
key_contribution: Introduces MMEarth, a global 1.2M-location multi-modal Sentinel-2-aligned pretraining dataset with 12 modalities, and proposes MP-MAE, a ConvNeXt V2-based fully convolutional multi-pretext masked autoencoder that improves fine-tuning and especially linear-probing performance over ImageNet and Sentinel-2-only MAE baselines

pretraining.objective: Multi-task masked reconstruction (MSE for continuous, cross-entropy for categorical) over pixel-level and image-level pretext targets

pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: Global, 1.2M locations sampled across 14 biomes, years 2017-2020

title: S2-CGAN: Self-Supervised Adversarial Representation Learning for Binary Change Detection in Multispectral Images
venue: IEEE International Geoscience and Remote Sensing Symposium
tldr: Proposes S2-cGAN, the first self-supervised conditional GAN that directly exploits the discriminator's likelihood to formulate binary change detection. Trained only on unchanged sample pairs with no labels for changed samples, the method achieves competitive performance against fully-supervised methods.
key_contribution: Proposes S2-cGAN, the first self-supervised conditional GAN that directly exploits the discriminator's likelihood to formulate binary change detection. Trained only on unchanged sample pairs with no labels for changed samples, the method achieves competitive performance against fully-supervised methods.

architecture.type: conditional GAN with U-Net generator and patch-based discriminator

pretraining.objective: adversarial training on unchanged sample reconstruction

title: Self-supervised pre-training enhances change detection in Sentinel-2 imagery
venue: ICPR Workshops
tldr: Introduces the Sentinel-2 Multitemporal Cities Pairs (S2MTCP) dataset of 1520 global urban image pairs and shows that self-supervised pretraining on this dataset (using overlap discrimination or triplet contrastive pretext tasks) enables state-of-the-art change detection on OSCD with minimal labeled data.
key_contribution: Introduces the Sentinel-2 Multitemporal Cities Pairs (S2MTCP) dataset of 1520 global urban image pairs and shows that self-supervised pretraining on this dataset (using overlap discrimination or triplet contrastive pretext tasks) enables state-of-the-art change detection on OSCD with minimal labeled data.

architecture.type: shallow Siamese CNN with three convolutional layers per branch and a fusion layer

pretraining.objective: overlap discrimination and triplet contrastive learning between patches

title: Self-Supervised Multisensor Change Detection
venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
tldr: Proposes a self-supervised method for multi-sensor (optical-SAR) change detection that requires only the bi-temporal target scene with no labels or additional unlabeled images. Combines deep clustering, temporal consistency loss, and contrastive loss in a Siamese architecture, outperforming prior unsupervised multisensor CD methods.
key_contribution: Proposes a self-supervised method for multi-sensor (optical-SAR) change detection that requires only the bi-temporal target scene with no labels or additional unlabeled images. Combines deep clustering, temporal consistency loss, and contrastive loss in a Siamese architecture, outperforming prior unsupervised multisensor CD methods.

architecture.type: two-branch (optical/SAR) CNN with shared common layer; non-shared projection modules and shared prediction module

pretraining.objective: deep clustering + temporal consistency + contrastive loss

datasets: target bi-temporal scenes only (Las Vegas, Chongqing, Abu Dhabi, Montpellier)
pretraining_data.num_images: single bi-temporal scene per experiment (few hundred px)
pretraining_data.geographic_coverage: four scenes (Las Vegas USA, Chongqing China, Abu Dhabi UAE, Montpellier France)

title: Self-supervised Change Detection in Multi-view Remote Sensing Images
venue: Remote Sensing Letters
tldr: Proposes a self-supervised change detection approach for multi-view remote sensing images using a pseudo-Siamese ResNet-34 trained with multi-view contrastive loss (heterogeneous case) and BYOL (homogeneous case). Introduces a multi-scale change scoring strategy and validates on five homogeneous and heterogeneous datasets.
key_contribution: Proposes a self-supervised change detection approach for multi-view remote sensing images using a pseudo-Siamese ResNet-34 trained with multi-view contrastive loss (heterogeneous case) and BYOL (homogeneous case). Introduces a multi-scale change scoring strategy and validates on five homogeneous and heterogeneous datasets.

architecture.type: pseudo-Siamese network (two branches with same architecture but unshared weights)

pretraining.objective: multi-view contrastive (InfoNCE) + BYOL regression

datasets: augmented OSCD subsets (S2S2, S1S1, L8S2, S1S2)

title: Task-Related Self-Supervised Learning for Remote Sensing Image Change Detection
venue: IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech, and Signal Processing
tldr: Introduces TSLCD, an unsupervised change detection framework combining a task-related self-supervised module (contrastive pretraining on segmentation-derived pseudo-labels), a hard-sample-mining loss, and a smooth mechanism. Achieves state-of-the-art unsupervised CD across four datasets from different sensors.
key_contribution: Introduces TSLCD, an unsupervised change detection framework combining a task-related self-supervised module (contrastive pretraining on segmentation-derived pseudo-labels), a hard-sample-mining loss, and a smooth mechanism. Achieves state-of-the-art unsupervised CD across four datasets from different sensors.

architecture.type: Siamese-style backbone with two FC layers for change classification

pretraining.objective: pairwise contrastive learning on segmented patches

datasets: target bi-temporal scenes (WV3 Site 1, WV3 Site 2, ZY3, QB)

title: Self-supervised Remote Sensing Images Change Detection at Pixel-level
tldr: First pixel-wise contrastive self-supervised approach for change detection in remote sensing. Combines a shift-equivariance contrastive pretext on a Siamese ResUnet with vector quantization and an uncertainty-aware teacher-student paradigm to suppress regular seasonal changes, improving over patch-based SSL CD.
key_contribution: First pixel-wise contrastive self-supervised approach for change detection in remote sensing. Combines a shift-equivariance contrastive pretext on a Siamese ResUnet with vector quantization and an uncertainty-aware teacher-student paradigm to suppress regular seasonal changes, improving over patch-based SSL CD.

architecture.type: Siamese encoder-decoder with vector quantization module

pretraining.objective: pixel-wise contrastive learning with shift equivariance + codebook loss + uncertainty estimation

title: Contrastive Multiview Coding with Electro-optics for SAR Semantic Segmentation
venue: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
tldr: First multi-modal contrastive multiview coding approach in remote sensing that uses an auxiliary EO sensor (and optionally segmentation maps) to pretrain SAR semantic segmentation models. Demonstrates that more modality views improve SAR building segmentation performance, sample efficiency, and convergence speed across multiple backbones and segmentation heads.
key_contribution: First multi-modal contrastive multiview coding approach in remote sensing that uses an auxiliary EO sensor (and optionally segmentation maps) to pretrain SAR semantic segmentation models. Demonstrates that more modality views improve SAR building segmentation performance, sample efficiency, and convergence speed across multiple backbones and segmentation heads.

architecture.type: contrastive multiview coding encoder (also evaluated with ResNeXt-50 and ResNeSt-50)

pretraining.objective: contrastive multiview coding across SAR, EO, and segmentation map

pretraining_data.num_images: scenes patched into 300x300 with 150 strides, resized to 448x448

title: Continual Barlow Twins: continual self-supervised learning for remote sensing semantic segmentation
venue: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
tldr: First combination of self-supervised learning and continual learning for remote sensing. Continual Barlow Twins integrates Barlow Twins with Elastic Weight Consolidation, enabling incremental pretraining on heterogeneous EO datasets (drone, aerial, satellite) without catastrophic forgetting, and proposes a new mixed-domain segmentation benchmark.
key_contribution: First combination of self-supervised learning and continual learning for remote sensing. Continual Barlow Twins integrates Barlow Twins with Elastic Weight Consolidation, enabling incremental pretraining on heterogeneous EO datasets (drone, aerial, satellite) without catastrophic forgetting, and proposes a new mixed-domain segmentation benchmark.

architecture.type: Barlow Twins encoder + projector with EWC regularization for continual learning; UNet++ for downstream segmentation

pretraining.objective: Barlow Twins redundancy reduction + EWC regularization

title: Self Supervised Learning for Few Shot Hyperspectral Image Classification
venue: IEEE International Geoscience and Remote Sensing Symposium
tldr: Demonstrates that Barlow-Twins-based self-supervised pretraining on unlabeled hyperspectral pixels significantly outperforms supervised baselines for few-shot HSI classification. Introduces patch/pixel pair sampling strategies that exploit spatial regularity and analyzes the impact of spatial vs. spectral data augmentations.
key_contribution: Demonstrates that Barlow-Twins-based self-supervised pretraining on unlabeled hyperspectral pixels significantly outperforms supervised baselines for few-shot HSI classification. Introduces patch/pixel pair sampling strategies that exploit spatial regularity and analyzes the impact of spatial vs. spectral data augmentations.

architecture.type: shared encoder + MLP projector for Barlow Twins

pretraining.objective: redundancy reduction (Barlow Twins) on HSI patches/pixels

pretraining_data.num_images: single HSI scenes (610x340 with 9 classes, 349x1905 with 144 bands and 15 classes)

title: Contrastive ground-level image and remote sensing pre-training improves representation learning for natural world imagery
venue: European Conference on Computer Vision
tldr: Introduces CRISP, an image-image contrastive pre-training approach pairing ground-level and aerial imagery for natural world representation learning, and the Nature Multi-View dataset (>3M pairs across 6,000+ California plant taxa). CRISP outperforms ImageNet, MAE, and MoCo baselines on fine-grained species recognition, especially in low-data regimes.
key_contribution: Introduces CRISP, an image-image contrastive pre-training approach pairing ground-level and aerial imagery for natural world representation learning, and the Nature Multi-View dataset (>3M pairs across 6,000+ California plant taxa). CRISP outperforms ImageNet, MAE, and MoCo baselines on fine-grained species recognition, especially in low-data regimes.

pretraining.objective: image-image contrastive (InfoNCE) between ground-level and aerial views

pretraining_data.num_images: >3 million ground-aerial image pairs from 1.75M observations
